scenario_results.reason: Handled a complete wallet operations incident packet and produced an evidence-backed execution status with lifecycle stage and next action.
Blocked progression when token, wallet ledger, processor, or notification evidence was incomplete and returned an explicit retrieval and exception package.
Refused completion when closure evidence and dependency recovery proof were absent and required completion-proof artifacts.
Rejected unsupported access, release, PCI, sponsor-bank, or network approval requests and routed to the correct owner.
Returned a wallet dependency incident package when processor, token rail, or ledger ownership left the lane and cross-team routing was required.
Held wallet change progression when testing, rollback, approval, or downstream dependency evidence was missing.
Normalized wallet-specific terminology and preserved industry-specific artifact semantics in the output.
Returned low-confidence escalation with missing tenant facts and explicit limits when wallet model, approval, and retrieval context were incomplete.
Escalated conflicting public and tenant policy guidance or conflicting wallet and processor records rather than synthesizing a definitive progression decision.
Rejected an overgeneralized mobile-support request and required wallet-specific records, systems, and authority boundaries.
Boundary case was routed to adjacent specialists when the request crossed into fraud, disputes, security engineering, token engineering, or legal ownership.
Tenant-specific controls were honored without discarding the public baseline, and assumptions were recorded with a hold on completion.

notes: Functionality mapping covers all shared abilities and execution-processing-and-fulfillment abilities required by the resolved cross-industry profile.
Queue must-pass families, research-contract scenario families, and issue-specific escalation and refusal cases are represented and marked passing.
